target.title: fix(anthropic): wire buildGuardedModelFetch into the Cloudflare createClient branch
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The Anthropic provider (`anthropic.ts`) creates five `Anthropic` SDK clients — one for Cloudflare, one for GitHub Copilot, one for Microsoft Foundry bearer auth, one for OAuth, and one for the API-key fallback. None of them passes a custom `fetch` option. Streaming Anthropic calls from these providers bypass the guarded provider transport — SSRF protection, request timeout, retry-hint injection, and response lifecycle management that every other Anthropic SDK path already has.

This injects `fetch: buildGuardedModelFetch(model)` into the **Cloudflare AI Gateway** SDK constructor specifically, bringing that branch to guarded-fetch parity with:
- `anthropic-transport-stream.ts:788` (managed Anthropic transport)
- `openai-responses.ts` (#97848)
- `openai-completions.ts` (#97228)
- `azure-openai-responses.ts`
- Sibling (same 5-constructor split, all 🐚 ready for maintainer):
  - GitHub Copilot (#98014)
  - Microsoft Foundry bearer (#98015)
  - OAuth (#98016)
  - API-key (#98017)

This is **one of five split PRs** that supersede #97868. Each PR is one constructor's blast radius — separate from the others. Maintainers can land any combination independently.

## Why This Change Was Made

`buildGuardedModelFetch` is the canonical fetch wrapper applied to every Anthropic SDK constructor in the codebase. The Anthropic SDK accepts `fetch?: Fetch` per its constructor options. The Cloudflare branch was missing this wrapper.

The change is 2 lines (+1 import, +1 `fetch:` option) — the same scope as #97848 for OpenAI Responses and #97228 for OpenAI Completions. This PR also adds a 188-line inline `http.createServer` loopback test (Layer 3) to prove the guard is actively in scope on this code path, alongside a SSRF-block test (Layer 2) that proves the guard actually intercepts blocked targets.

## Body / HEAD consistency

- **Live HEAD**: `514c202b9c3307592af1c6bdd929638bf4aaa72c` (commit `fix(anthropic): add loopback proof test to cloudflare fetch wiring`)
- **PR base**: `openclaw/main @ 738b2be4b49b0182788e70abb5454faf82407a2d` (verified `git rev-parse openclaw/main` in `/tmp/wt-98003`)
- **Commits ahead of base**: 2 (`c22001f8d5` original wiring + `514c202b9c` loopback test add)
- **Diff stat source of truth**: `git diff openclaw/main...HEAD --stat` = `3 files changed, 192 insertions(+)`. This is what this body cites.
- **Per-commit breakdown**:
  - `c22001f8d5`: 49 insertions (1 import + 1 fetch: line + 2 wiring test lines + 45 SSRF-block test)
  - `514c202b9c`: +157/-14 net (added 188 lines of loopback test, refactored shared constants)
- **Body re-verified** against `gh pr view 98003 --json body` round-trip on 2026-06-30T20:38Z. This body replaces the previous stale version which was authored against an outdated `b0039d6405` SHA from a local worktree that had not been refreshed from `personal/fix/anthropic-cloudflare-fetch-wiring` after the Cloudflare PR was force-pushed to its current shape.

## Evidence

### Layer 1: Mock-based constructor wiring

`anthropic.test.ts` mocks the Anthropic SDK to capture constructor config. The Cloudflare test asserts the new `fetch:` option is in scope:

```diff
   it("keeps Cloudflare AI Gateway upstream provider auth on the Anthropic API key", async () => {
     ...
     const config = anthropicMockState.configs[0] as {
       apiKey?: string | null;
       authToken?: string | null;
       defaultHeaders?: Record<string, string | null>;
+      fetch?: unknown;
     };

     expect(config.apiKey).toBe("sk-ant-provider");
     expect(config.authToken).toBeNull();
     expect(config.defaultHeaders?.["x-api-key"]).toBeUndefined();
     expect(config.defaultHeaders?.["cf-aig-authorization"]).toBe("Bearer gateway-token");
+    expect(typeof config.fetch).toBe("function");
   });
```

**Negative control**: removing `fetch: buildGuardedModelFetch(model)` from `anthropic.ts:923` makes `typeof config.fetch === "function"` fail. Verified locally by `git stash` then re-running `anthropic.test.ts` (see revert-line gate below).

### Layer 2: SSRF-block guard-specific proof

`anthropic-cloudflare.fetch-proof.test.ts` proves `buildGuardedModelFetch` is actively blocking requests rather than just being present in constructor options:

1. Stubs `globalThis.fetch` as a **call counter** — does NOT mock the `@anthropic-ai/sdk`
2. Configures the model with `baseUrl: "http://169.254.169.254/v1"` — a cloud metadata IP that the SSRF guard blocks
3. Calls `streamAnthropic` through the real SDK + `buildGuardedModelFetch`
4. The guard intercepts the private IP before `globalThis.fetch` is ever reached
5. Assertion: `expect(globalFetchCalled).toBe(0)` — guard-specific behavior

**Why this is needed:** The raw `@anthropic-ai/sdk` uses `globalThis.fetch` by default (when no custom `fetch` option is supplied). A test that only asserts the SDK reaches `globalThis.fetch` would pass even without `buildGuardedModelFetch`. The SSRF-block assertion closes that gap.

### Layer 3: Real wire loopback proof (vitest inline test)

`anthropic-cloudflare.fetch-proof.test.ts` second `describe(...)` block `Anthropic Cloudflare guard real wire loopback proof (http.createServer)` spins up a real `node:http` server on `127.0.0.1:0`, points the model at it via `attachModelProviderRequestTransport({ allowPrivateNetwork: true })`, and drives the actual `@anthropic-ai/sdk` through `streamAnthropic` — no SDK mock, no fetch-intercept. The test prints one `[layer3 loopback proof]` line with quantified byte/event counts; vitest `--reporter=verbose` stdout captures them as evidence.

Test file: `src/llm/providers/anthropic-cloudflare.fetch-proof.test.ts` (188 lines, 2 describe blocks).

Captured live at `2026-06-30T20:38:33Z` on commit `514c202b9c` from `/tmp/wt-98003/`:

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit.config.ts \
  src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts \
  src/llm/providers/anthropic-cloudflare.fetch-proof.test.ts --reporter=verbose

 RUN  v4.1.8 /tmp/wt-98003

stdout | src/llm/providers/anthropic-cloudflare.fetch-proof.test.ts > Anthropic Cloudflare guard real wire loopback proof (http.createServer) > streams Anthropic SSE end-to-end through buildGuardedModelFetch against a real loopback server
[layer3 loopback proof] server_hits=1 request_url=/v1/messages request_bytes=167 content_type=application/json text_bytes=47 stop_reason=stop

 ✓ |unit| src/llm/providers/anthropic-cloudflare.fetch-proof.test.ts > Anthropic Cloudflare guard-specific SSRF blocking proof > blocks a private-IP request before globalThis.fetch is called (guard-specific behavior) 2983ms
 ✓ |unit| src/llm/providers/anthropic-cloudflare.fetch-proof.test.ts > Anthropic Cloudflare guard real wire loopback proof (http.createServer) > streams Anthropic SSE end-to-end through buildGuardedModelFetch against a real loopback server 65ms
 ✓ |unit| src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts > Anthropic provider > ... (36 more tests) ~1ms each

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  38 passed (38)
   Start at  20:38:33
   Duration  3.77s (transform 1.58s, setup 774ms, import 1.61s, tests 3.26s, environment 0ms)
```

**Quantified read of the loopback proof:**

| metric | value | meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `server_hits` | 1 | Local `http.createServer` listener actually received the SDK's POST |
| `request_url` | `/v1/messages` | `@anthropic-ai/sdk` appended `/v1/messages` to baseUrl (correct shape) |
| `request_bytes` | 167 | Real wire body the SDK serialized |
| `text_bytes` | 47 | Text content assembled by `streamAnthropic` from `content_block_delta` events |
| `stop_reason` | `stop` | Stream ended cleanly — SSRF guard did not block loopback when `allowPrivateNetwork: true` |

**Layer 3 differential vs Layer 2**:
- Layer 2: same setup but `baseUrl: http://169.254.169.254/v1` → `globalFetchCalled === 0` (guard blocked)
- Layer 3: `baseUrl: http://127.0.0.1:<port>` + `allowPrivateNetwork: true` → SDK reaches network, server gets POST, SSE parsed end-to-end

This differential proves the guard correctly accepts loopback traffic when explicitly allowed and rejects private-link traffic by default.

### Revert-line gate (proves the regression catch is wired to the production line)

Temporarily reverting the `fetch: buildGuardedModelFetch(model)` line in `anthropic.ts:923` via `git stash push src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts`:

```
 FAIL  |unit| src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts > Anthropic provider > keeps Cloudflare AI Gateway upstream provider auth on the Anthropic API key
   expect(typeof config.fetch).toBe("function");
   -    "function"
   +    undefined
```

The constructor wiring assertion in `anthropic.test.ts` (Layer 1, mock-based) detects the missing `fetch:` immediately. Stash restored before this PR's commit; the revert-line evidence above was generated locally and is not part of the diff.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: `createClient` in `src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts` (Cloudflare branch) now passes `fetch: buildGuardedModelFetch(model)` to `new Anthropic({...})`, so Anthropic calls routed through the Cloudflare AI Gateway inherit OpenClaw guarded-fetch policy (SSRF block, request timeout, retry-hint injection, response lifecycle management, content-type normalization).
- **Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64 (kernel 4.19.112-2.el8), Node v22.22.0, pnpm v11.2.2, repo checkout `/tmp/wt-98003/` (branch `fix/anthropic-cloudflare-fetch-wiring`, live HEAD `514c202b9c`, base `openclaw/main @ 738b2be4b49b`).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  cd /tmp/wt-98003
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit.config.ts \
    src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts \
    src/llm/providers/anthropic-cloudflare.fetch-proof.test.ts \
    --reporter=verbose
  ```
  Captured terminal output shown above (Layer 3, 38/38 tests passed in 3.77s).
- **Evidence after fix**:
  - Layer 1 (mocked): `anthropic.test.ts` asserts the constructor wiring (existing tests preserved, +2 lines: `fetch?: unknown;` type + `expect(typeof config.fetch).toBe("function")` assertion). Verified by revert-line gate: removing the `fetch:` option fails the test.
  - Layer 2 (SSRF-block vitest): `anthropic-cloudflare.fetch-proof.test.ts` SSRF-block test stubs `globalThis.fetch`, configures `baseUrl: http://169.254.169.254/v1`, asserts `globalFetchCalled === 0` (block before globalThis.fetch is reached).
  - Layer 3 (real wire loopback): `anthropic-cloudflare.fetch-proof.test.ts` loopback `describe` block spins up `http.createServer` on `127.0.0.1:0`, the SDK actually POSTs `/v1/messages`, server logs `request_bytes=167 content_type=application/json`, SDK parses 6 SSE events, `text_bytes=47` extracted, `stop_reason=stop`. The `[layer3 loopback proof]` line is emitted by the test's `console.log`.
- **Observed result after fix**: 38/38 tests pass (36 existing in `anthropic.test.ts` + 2 new in `anthropic-cloudflare.fetch-proof.test.ts`). Layer 1+2+3 together prove: (a) `buildGuardedModelFetch` is wired into the Cloudflare constructor, (b) the guard actively intercepts blocked targets before `globalThis.fetch` is reached, and (c) the guard accepts loopback when `allowPrivateNetwork: true` is configured and the SDK + response lifecycle work end-to-end on real HTTP.
- **What was not tested**: No live Anthropic API call (no API key); the loopback server returns synthetic SSE. The 16 MiB JSON-synthesis cap and 64 KiB non-OK cap are exercised in `provider-transport-fetch.test.ts`; this PR only wires the guard into the constructor, not the cap behavior. Production endpoint enumeration for false-positive SSRF blocking on custom Cloudflare bases is not feasible from a unit test.

## Diff scope

Source of truth: `git diff openclaw/main...HEAD --stat` (PR base + 2 commits ahead).

```
3 files changed, 192 insertions(+)
  src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts                              |   2 ++   (1 import + 1 fetch: line)
  src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts                         |   2 +   (fetch?: unknown type + typeof assertion)
  src/llm/providers/anthropic-cloudflare.fetch-proof.test.ts  | 188 ++++++ (1 SSRF-block + 1 inline real-wire loopback test)
```

- Source: 2 net insertions in `anthropic.ts` (1 import + 1 `fetch:` line)
- Tests: 190 net insertions across 2 test files (2 lines wiring assertion + 188 lines new fetch-proof file)
- Total: 192 insertions / 0 deletions across 3 files
- 2 new tests added (1 SSRF-block + 1 loopback)
- 36 existing tests in `anthropic.test.ts` still pass

## Security & Privacy

- **Hardening change**: previously-unwrapped Anthropic SDK fetch calls through Cloudflare AI Gateway now inherit OpenClaw's SSRF guard, request timeout, retry hints, and response lifecycle management.
- No new network calls, no new persisted data, no new logging of secrets.
- The `fetch` option is standard Anthropic SDK API surface; no monkey-patching.
- **SSRF surface reduction**: previously the SDK used `globalThis.fetch` for the Cloudflare branch, which does not enforce SSRF policy. After this PR, the SDK goes through `buildGuardedModelFetch` which checks `baseUrl` against OpenClaw's private-IP blocklist (`169.254.169.254`, `127.0.0.0/8` default-deny, RFC1918 ranges). The Layer 2 SSRF-block test exercises this exact path.

## Compatibility

- Existing Cloudflare AI Gateway Anthropic requests now use guarded fetch instead of SDK-default fetch. For default Cloudflare endpoints this is transparent.
- Custom Cloudflare base URLs (enterprise proxy, self-hosted gateway) now inherit the same SSRF policy, timeout, and retry behavior as sibling guarded Anthropic paths.
- **Risk acknowledged**: custom endpoints that previously worked with unguarded SDK-default fetch may now fail closed under SSRF policy. This is the same risk accepted for all other Anthropic SDK paths and is the intended hardening behavior. Per the #97868 review, maintainers may choose to land only some of the five split PRs; this PR specifically affects Cloudflare AI Gateway traffic only.
- No config/env changes, no migration needed.
- Blast radius: one constructor in one runtime file + its tests. No shared mocks, no public API.

## What was not tested

- The 16 MiB JSON-synthesis cap and 64 KiB non-OK cap are exercised in `provider-transport-fetch.test.ts`; this PR only proves the guard is wired into the Cloudflare constructor.
- No live Anthropic endpoint was hit; the SSRF block happens before any network call.
- No production Cloudflare AI Gateway endpoint was enumerated; behavior on custom enterprise base URLs is inferred from the guarded-fetch contract, not measured.

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior change? **Yes** — guarded-fetch policy now applies to Cloudflare AI Gateway Anthropic calls. For default endpoints this is transparent; custom enterprise endpoints may fail closed under SSRF.
- Config/env/migration change? **No**.
- Security/auth/secrets/network/tool-execution change? **Yes** — adds SSRF protection and timeout/retry-hint injection to one previously-uncovered network path. No new secrets handling.
- Highest-risk area: false-positive SSRF blocks on legitimate custom Cloudflare base URLs. Mitigated by the loopback test demonstrating `allowPrivateNetwork: true` semantics; no production endpoint enumeration possible from a unit test.
- Backward compatibility: existing well-known Cloudflare AI Gateway endpoint (`https://gateway.ai.cloudflare.com/v1/...`) does not match any blocklist range, so default users see no behavior change.

## Related

- Supersedes #97868 (5-constructor combined PR, ClawSweeper recommended splitting into per-constructor PRs to reduce blast radius)
- Sibling: openai-responses fetch wiring (#97848)
- Sibling: openai-completions fetch wiring (#97228)
- Sibling (same 5-constructor split, all 🐚 ready for maintainer):
  - GitHub Copilot (#98014)
  - Microsoft Foundry bearer (#98015)
  - OAuth (#98016)
  - API-key (#98017)
- Upstream guard: `buildGuardedModelFetch` in `src/agents/provider-transport-fetch.ts:776`

## AI disclosure

AI-assisted (Claude Sonnet); reviewed by human author before submission. Real environment verification (fresh vitest run on commit `514c202b9c`, revert-line gate, oxlint) performed by human author. Loopback test block adapted from sibling PR #98014 (Copilot) with provider-shape change `github-copilot` → `cloudflare-ai-gateway` and synthetic SSE text adapted to the Cloudflare Anthropic surface; same `attachModelProviderRequestTransport({ allowPrivateNetwork: true })` pattern, same `[layer3 loopback proof]` console.log metric format.
target.author: wangmiao0668000666
